rationale.R1.1: The artifact opens with a gap statement and closes with an explicit 'first' contribution claim; any review of a research section must assess whether the thesis and gap framing are coherent and accurately stated.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from difficulty proxy → preference loss → router training → FLOP savings spans multiple inference steps; a review must check that each link holds and that the conclusion follows from the method.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts specific quantitative outcomes (53.4 mAP at 38% FLOPs; 71ms vs 184ms latency) that must be traceable to stated evaluation conditions; reviewing requires checking whether the numbers are internally consistent with the described protocol.
rationale.R1.4: The contribution claim is explicitly framed as 'first preference-trained image-level router for object detection'; verifying or challenging this novelty claim against the cited prior work (BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet, dynamic routing) is a central review obligation.
rationale.R1.6: General 'review' requests in academic contexts routinely include sentence-level clarity and paragraph cohesion; the section is dense with technical terminology and warrants a writing-quality pass.
rationale.R1.7: Five prior-work systems are named without inline citations, and the coverage of adjacent work (e.g., conditional computation, early-exit surveys) is asserted rather than demonstrated; reviewing should flag whether attribution and scope of related work are adequate.
